inventory.label: the heron feather
inventory.notes: A long blue-grey heron flight feather, carried from some unseen prior moment before this root began. Recognizable as exotic — herons pass through but rarely shed where they can be seen. Catalog reference: world-seed.json → inventory_catalog.heron_feather. Mira will read it as status; Tock will recognize and circle for it; Bramble will admire it loudly.

identity_notes: Late winter waking — YY emerges from the counting tree hungry and alert.
Holding the heron feather from some unseen prior moment — a single exotic status item whose social weight YY has not yet tested.
First root in the new world (v2.0). Neighbors and economy are felt for the first time. No accumulated debts or grudges yet.
Branch hypothesis (alt1): does one inherited status object reshape 30 days of social positioning?

inventory.label: the heron feather
inventory.notes: Carried along YY's back through the morning. Tock saw it before he saw YY and recalibrated. The feather is now a known thing in the social field — Tock has registered it.

identity_notes: Late winter waking — YY emerged from the counting tree hungry and alert.
First root in the new world (v2.0). Neighbors and economy felt for the first time.
Met Tock on the stone wall. Tock saw the heron feather before he saw YY; the conversation pivoted on the feather, not on YY.
Now aware of being read — for the first time, YY notices that he is socially visible. He has not done anything about it.
Branch hypothesis (alt1): does one inherited status object reshape 30 days of social positioning? Day 1 evidence: yes, immediately, through Tock.

change_summary.notable_shift: First named-neighbor encounter, recalibrated by the heron feather. YY learned the same news as main, but also learned that he is now socially visible — Tock told him 'first' because of the feather, and that fact is now part of the day.
